Tests the handling of new lines in string literals using JavaScript's eval function.

# Purpose
This code is a test script that verifies the behavior of the `eval` function in JavaScript when processing string literals with escaped newline characters. It contains two assertions that check if the `eval` function correctly interprets and concatenates strings with escaped carriage return and newline sequences. The first assertion tests a string with a carriage return and newline (`'1\\\r\n2'`), while the second tests a string with a newline (`'1\\\n2'`). Both assertions expect the evaluated result to be the concatenated string `'12'`. This script ensures that the `eval` function handles these specific escape sequences as expected.
